title: Rosehip and frankincense night cream
subtitle: A water-in-oil emulsion with rosehip and jojoba oils, frankincense essential oil, and a broad-spectrum preservative
excerpt: This night cream uses emulsifying wax to combine a water phase and an oil phase into a stable emulsion. Rosehip oil contributes vitamin A and linoleic acid; jojoba adds a lightweight slip. Frankincense essential oil gives a resinous scent. Because the formulation contains water, a broad-spectrum preservative is required. Shelf life is 3 months at room temperature.
sourceNotes: Emulsification basics: water and oil phases must both be heated to 70 to 75°C before combining to ensure the emulsifying wax forms a stable emulsion (reference: Making Natural Cosmetics, Anne-Marie Faiola, 2020). Emulsifying wax (INCI: Emulsifying Wax NF or Polawax): used at 6 to 8% of water phase. Rosehip oil (Rosa canina): high in linoleic acid and beta-carotene; relatively short shelf life (6 months for the oil alone); antioxidant addition of vitamin E extends shelf life in the formulation. Preservative system: broad-spectrum preservative at 1% of total batch weight prevents mould and bacterial growth in a water-containing emulsion. Frankincense EO (Boswellia carterii): 0.5% in a leave-on facial product is within typical usage ranges.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 190 ml (fills 2 x 100 ml amber glass bottles)
recipe.batchNotes: The emulsification step requires consistent temperature control; batch scaling is possible but requires a larger vessel and a stick blender or hand mixer to maintain shear.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow the emulsion to cool fully before jarring. The cream will thicken as it reaches room temperature.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months at room temperature with the preservative. Refrigerate to extend to 4 to 6 months. Discard immediately if the cream changes colour, smells off, or the emulsion breaks (appears greasy and watery at the same time).

glossaryTerms.definition: The process of combining two immiscible liquids, water and oil, into a stable mixture using an emulsifying agent. The emulsifier coats tiny droplets of one phase within the other, preventing them from separating. Heating both phases to the same temperature before combining is essential for a stable emulsion.

A cosmetic preservative that inhibits both bacterial and fungal growth. Required in any water-containing formulation. Without a broad-spectrum preservative, a cream or lotion can develop mould or bacterial contamination within days.

content.text: This cream combines a water phase and an oil phase into a stable mixture. Both phases must reach 70 to 75°C before they go together; then the blend is beaten as it cools to complete the

: without one the cream can grow bacteria or mould within days. Rosehip oil adds a rich, nourishing oil phase; frankincense at 0.5% gives a resinous scent and is safe for leave-on facial use. Patch test before applying to the face.

items.prepNote: Water phase; use distilled or deionised water only
Oil phase; Polawax or Emulsifying Wax NF; do not substitute with beeswax
Oil phase; use fresh oil; rosehip oxidises relatively quickly
Antioxidant; added with the preservative and fragrance at 40°C
1% of total batch weight; add at 40°C or below; follow manufacturer instructions
0.5% of total batch weight; add at 40°C

content.text: Set up two bain-marie vessels. Place the distilled water in one; place the emulsifying wax, rosehip oil, and jojoba oil in the other. Heat both to 75°C simultaneously, checking with the thermometer. Once both phases are at 75°C, they are ready to combine.

Pour the hot water phase slowly into the oil phase, stirring continuously with the hand mixer on a low setting. The mixture will turn opaque and cream-like as the emulsion forms. Continue mixing as the cream cools, scraping down the sides of the bowl. The emulsion must be kept in motion as it cools to set the emulsification.

Once the cream has cooled to 40°C, add the vitamin E oil, broad-spectrum preservative, and frankincense essential oil. Stir to combine thoroughly. Do not add the preservative above 40°C; heat degrades most preservative systems.

Decant the cream into the amber glass bottles while still fluid; it will thicken as it reaches room temperature. Label with the batch date and use-by date (3 months from today). Leave overnight to reach full thickness before use.

Store in a cool, dark place or in the refrigerator. With the preservative, the cream keeps 3 months at room temperature and up to 6 months refrigerated. Discard immediately if the cream changes colour, develops an off smell, or if the emulsion breaks and you see separate oily and watery layers. Patch test before first use.
